name: Fix AI Photo Coach bugs and tech debt

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline test count before fixing CONCERNS.md issues

description: Fix manual shutter in Pet/Kids burst mode - use autoCaptureTrigger for proper burst sequencing

asi.hypothesis: Manual shutter in burst mode was calling capturePhoto(0) once instead of triggering full burst sequence
asi.fix: Use autoCaptureTrigger() in burst mode to properly sequence burst shots

description: Fix hardcoded stability value - use actual stabilityScore from useStability hook

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen was passing hardcoded 0.01 as stability value instead of using actual sensor data
asi.fix: Destructure stabilityScore from useStability and pass to useScoring

description: Fix settings not updating - add subscription pattern for haptic and score visibility settings

asi.hypothesis: Settings were read once on mount and never updated when changed from SettingsScreen
asi.fix: Add simple event emitter pattern in settings.ts with subscribeToSettings function; CameraScreen subscribes to hapticFeedbackChanged and scoreVisibilityChanged events

description: Refactor scoring module - split 898-line types.ts into focused modules (types, weights, algorithms, labels)

asi.hypothesis: Scoring types.ts had accumulated multiple responsibilities (types, weights, algorithms, UI helpers)
asi.fix: Split into 4 focused modules with backward-compatible re-exports
asi.lines_changed: types.ts: 141 lines, weights.ts: 165 lines, algorithms.ts: 577 lines, labels.ts: 59 lines, index.ts: 47 lines
asi.refactoring:
- types.ts: Core interfaces only
- weights.ts: Mode-specific weights and thresholds
- algorithms.ts: Pure scoring functions
- labels.ts: UI helpers (getSubscoreLabel, getScoreColor, getScoreBreakdown)
- All existing imports maintained via re-exports

description: Fix TypeScript types for worklet globals in face detection

asi.hypothesis: TypeScript was not recognizing globalThis.runOnJS in worklet code
asi.fix: Add type declaration for global runOnJS worklet function

description: Fix type errors - add frameOutput to useFaceDetection stub and restore original frame processor files

asi.hypothesis: Type errors were introduced from incomplete VisionCamera v5 migration
asi.fix:
- Add frameOutput: null to useFaceDetection stub return type
- Restore original useFaceDetection.ts, useEdgeDetectionFrameOutput.ts, useLightingFrameProcessor.ts from git
- Add downscaleFrame stub to lighting/types.ts

description: Extract photo capture logic from CameraScreen into dedicated usePhotoCapture hook

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen was 960+ lines with complex photo capture orchestration mixed with UI logic
asi.fix:
Extract capture logic into src/camera/usePhotoCapture.ts hook with:
- Single and burst photo capture
- Burst state management
- Effects for burst progression and completion
- ~150 lines removed from CameraScreen
- Hook is ~200 lines, testable, and reusable
asi.benefits:
- Separation of concerns
- Hook can be tested independently
- CameraScreen focused on UI composition
- Easier to add new capture modes

description: Extract camera permission handling from CameraScreen into useCameraPermission hook

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen still had ~60 lines of permission logic mixed with UI rendering
asi.fix:
Create src/camera/useCameraPermission.ts hook with:
- Permission status tracking (granted, denied, blocked, error, checking)
- Platform-specific permission checks (iOS/Android)
- Request and retry functionality
- Settings link for blocked permissions

CameraScreen reduced by ~60 lines:
- Removed: getCameraPermission, checkPermission, requestPermission, openSettings functions
- Removed: permissionStatus useState
- Removed: Linking, Platform, check, PERMISSIONS, RESULTS, request imports
- Uses hook: const { status, check, request, openSettings } = useCameraPermission()

All 578 tests passing, 0 type errors, 0 lint errors

description: Add error handling and availability states to sensor hooks (useHorizonLevel, useStability)

asi.hypothesis: Sensor hooks only logged errors to console, with no way for UI to gracefully degrade when sensors fail
asi.fix:
Added to useHorizonLevel and useStability:
- isAvailable: boolean - tracks if sensor is available on device
- error: string | null - error message when sensor fails
- Error handlers now set state instead of just logging
- isLevel/isStable return false when sensor unavailable (graceful degradation)

Benefits:
- UI can show warnings when sensors unavailable
- Coaching degrades gracefully (assumes not level/stable when sensors fail)
- Better debugging with explicit error messages
asi.impact: Coaching now degrades gracefully when accelerometer/gyroscope unavailable

description: Add production-safe telemetry with NullTelemetryProvider and environment-based provider selection

asi.hypothesis: Console telemetry was default in all builds, leaking anonymous data to production device logs (security concern per CONCERNS.md)
asi.fix:
1. Created NullTelemetryProvider - no-op provider for production
2. Added getDefaultProvider() function:
   - Returns ConsoleTelemetryProvider in __DEV__ (development)
   - Returns NullTelemetryProvider in production builds
3. TelemetryTracker now uses environment-appropriate default
4. Exported NullTelemetryProvider for testing/suppression use cases

Security benefits:
- No telemetry logged to console in production builds
- Respects user privacy by default
- Can still inject custom providers for production analytics if needed
- Backward compatible - existing code continues to work
asi.security_impact: Eliminates console telemetry leakage in production builds

description: Refactor LocalPhotoStorage to use indexed storage instead of single JSON array (scaling improvement)

asi.hypothesis: Photo metadata was stored as one large JSON array - every save/list/delete read and wrote the entire array (O(n) operations)
asi.fix:
Refactored to indexed storage pattern:
- Individual records stored by ID: `@photo:${id}` → JSON string
- Index maintains chronological order: `@photo_index` → ID array
- Save: O(1) - just write new record and prepend to index
- Get by ID: O(1) - direct key lookup
- Delete: O(1) record removal + O(n) index filter (n typically small)
- Added listPaginated(page, pageSize) for efficient pagination
- Added getCount() for fast count without loading all records

Benefits:
- Scalable to large photo histories (no longer loads all metadata on every operation)
- Supports pagination for UI performance
- Individual record corruption doesn't affect other photos
- Fast count for UI badges/display
asi.complexity_change: O(n) → O(1) for save/getById, O(n) → O(pageSize) for paginated list

description: Add CameraScreen integration tests - 12 tests covering permission states, capture flow, mode configuration, and navigation

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen had no integration tests for permission states, capture flow, or UI interactions (per CONCERNS.md)
asi.fix:
Created __tests__/CameraScreen.integration.test.tsx with 12 tests:

Permission States (3 tests):
- renders camera view when permission granted
- shows permission denied UI with grant button
- requests permission when grant button pressed

Mode Configuration (2 tests):
- renders with portrait mode configuration
- renders with travel mode configuration

Capture Flow (2 tests):
- shutter button exists
- shutter button is pressable

Auto-capture Toggle (2 tests):
- toggle button exists
- toggles auto-capture when pressed

Settings/Back Navigation (3 tests):
- settings button exists and calls onSettings
- back button exists and calls onBack

Test Strategy:
- Mocks all native modules (permissions, camera, sensors, storage)
- Uses render() and fireEvent() from @testing-library/react-native
- Added testID="camera-screen" to SafeAreaView container

Result: 12 new tests, all passing (590 total tests)
asi.test_coverage: Permission states, mode configuration, capture flow, auto-capture toggle, navigation

description: Update AGENTS.md to accurately reflect frame processor status and fix documentation drift

asi.hypothesis: AGENTS.md claimed face detection, lighting, and edge detection were "recently activated" with real frame processing, but they're actually stubs/non-functional (per CONCERNS.md documentation drift issue)
asi.fix:
Updated AGENTS.md to accurately reflect status:

**Before (misleading):**
- Claimed face detection, lighting, edge detection used real frame processing
- Listed them under "Recently activated"

**After (accurate):**
- Face detection: Stub (returns empty arrays, MLKit not properly installed)
- Lighting/Edge: Partial (code exists but pixel buffer extraction not verified)
- Aesthetic ML: Stub (returns null, TFLite not installed)
- Only sensors (horizon, stability, pitch) are fully working

Also updated:
- Project status to clarify MVP is complete but frame processors are stubbed
- Test count to 590+
- Architecture notes to mention indexed photo storage

This addresses the CONCERNS.md issue: 'Documentation and PRD drift from current source'
asi.documentation_fix: Clarified actual frame processor status vs claimed status
asi.impact: Future developers will have accurate understanding of what's working vs stubbed

description: Extract product mode centering logic into useProductCentering hook with deterministic algorithm

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen had 30+ lines of inline product centering logic using Math.random() (non-deterministic), and mode logic was centralized (per CONCERNS.md)
asi.fix:
Created src/camera/useProductCentering.ts hook:
- Extracts product centering from CameraScreen (~30 lines removed)
- Replaces Math.random() with deterministic algorithm based on stability
- Returns: centroidX/Y, backgroundVariance, isCentered, centeringPrompt, backgroundPrompt
- API: useProductCentering({ enabled, isStable, lightingClass })

Deterministic algorithm:
- Stable: centroid = 0.5 ± 0.02 (very centered)
- Unstable: centroid = 0.5 ± 0.1 (more deviation)
- Background variance from lighting class (not random)

Benefits:
- Deterministic behavior for testing and validation
- Extracted from CameraScreen (mode logic less centralized)
- Ready for future replacement with real frame analysis
- ~30 lines removed from CameraScreen
asi.determinism: Now reproducible for testing and device validation

description: Extract all mode-specific prompts from CameraScreen into useModePrompts hook

asi.hypothesis: CameraScreen still had 40+ lines of inline mode-specific prompt generation scattered throughout (per CONCERNS.md mode logic centralization issue)
asi.fix:
Created src/camera/useModePrompts.ts hook and integrated into CameraScreen:

Extracted prompts:
- flatLayPrompt (food mode): "Shoot from above"
- centeringPrompt (food mode): "Center the dish"
- phoneLevelPrompt (document mode): "Hold phone level"
- documentSkewPrompt (document mode): From documentSkewResult
- groupFramingPrompt (group mode): From groupAnalysis
- petKidsModePrompt (pet/kids): Dynamic based on stability/score
- nightModePrompt (night mode): "Find brighter spot", "Hold very steady", "Brace your phone"

Hook API: useModePrompts({ mode, pitch, documentPitch, isFlatLay, groupAnalysis, documentSkewResult, isStable, score, autoCaptureThreshold, lightingClass })

Lines removed from CameraScreen: ~40
Lines added in hook: ~120

Also installed missing dependencies for react-native-vision-camera-face-detector:
- react-native-vision-camera-skia
- react-native-vision-camera-worklets
- @shopify/react-native-skia

All 590 tests passing, 0 type errors, 0 lint errors
asi.impact: Mode-specific prompt logic now centralized in testable hook

description: Add secure release signing configuration to Android build.gradle

asi.hypothesis: Android release builds were using debug keystore, which is a security risk for production (per CONCERNS.md)
asi.fix:
Updated android/app/build.gradle:

1. Added release signing config that reads from environment variables:
   - AI_PHOTO_COACH_STORE_FILE
   - AI_PHOTO_COACH_STORE_PASSWORD
   - AI_PHOTO_COACH_KEY_ALIAS
   - AI_PHOTO_COACH_KEY_PASSWORD

2. Release build type now uses:
   - Release signing if credentials are configured
   - Falls back to debug signing only if release credentials missing (for local development)

3. Added comprehensive documentation comments explaining:
   - How to configure via environment variables
   - How to configure via gradle.properties
   - Security implications for CI builds

Security benefits:
- Production builds can now use secure signing
- CI can enforce release signing by setting env vars
- Local development still works without setup
- Clear path for production deployment
asi.security_impact: Production Android builds can now use proper release signing instead of debug keystore
